description: Prune test/ExecutionProxy.t.sol so it compiles and passes against the pure-VM ExecutionProxy from INF-0002. Delete every test that exercises removed surface: fee tests (Step 6 section), slippage checks, rescue function, Ownable access, EIP-712 signed fee overrides, OutputSpec-based execute(), executeSingle(), ReentrancyGuard tests targeting ExecutionProxy (reentrancy is now Router's concern). Keep tests that validate Weiroll command/state execution mechanics by rewriting them against the new executePath(commands, state) entry point. Strip MockWETH usage tied to wrap/unwrap slippage if those tests cannot be meaningfully restated against the pure VM; prefer keeping a minimal wrap/unwrap Weiroll flow test. All Router-specific behavior (fees, slippage, balance-diff, adversarial-token accounting) is covered separately in INF-0007..INF-0012 and must NOT be restated in this file.
commit: test(execution-proxy): prune fee/slippage tests for pure-VM refactor
steps: Open test/ExecutionProxy.t.sol and delete the Fee Tests section (anchored by the `// Fee Tests (Step 6)` banner comment) through end of file, along with all helper methods used only by fee tests (_signFeeOverrideForProxy, _computeExecutionHashSingle, _computeExecutionHashMulti, _buildMintProgramForProxy).

Delete slippage-focused tests: test_ExecuteSingle_SlippageExceeded, test_Execute_MultiOutputSlippageExceededOnSecond, test_Slippage_ZeroMinZeroBalance, test_Slippage_ExactMatch, test_Slippage_OneWeiBelow, test_Slippage_MaxUint256, test_ExecuteSingle_NativeETHSlippageExceeded, testFuzz_SlippageBoundary.
Delete rescue tests: test_Rescue, test_RescueETH, test_Rescue_OnlyOwner, test_Rescue_PartialAmount, test_Rescue_ToZeroAddress.
Delete reentrancy tests against ExecutionProxy: test_ExecuteSingle_ReentrancyViaETH, test_Execute_ReentrancyViaTokenCallback (they move to Router.Adversarial.t.sol in INF-0011).

Delete the MockERC20 allowance/approval tests that relied on receiver/output flow through the old entry points. Rewrite remaining Weiroll-execution tests (test_ExecuteSingle_WithApproveAndTransfer, test_Execute_WithMockDEXSwap, test_ExecuteSingle_WithWETHWrap, test_ExecuteSingle_WithWETHUnwrap, test_Execute_MultiHopSwap) to call `proxy.executePath(commands, state)` with commands that mint/transfer directly to an arbitrary address; assert via token balanceOf rather than via returned actualAmounts.
Update setUp(): drop the 4-arg ExecutionProxy constructor call; instantiate with `new ExecutionProxy()`. Remove feeSignerPk/feeSignerAddr.
Run `forge test --match-contract ExecutionProxyTest -vvv` and confirm all remaining tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: [auto] grep -cn 'proxy.executePath(' test/ExecutionProxy.t.sol is at least 3 (the retained Weiroll-flow tests all call the new entry point).
[auto] Test file compiles: `forge build --match-path test/ExecutionProxy.t.sol` returns no errors.
[manual] At least one retained test exercises a realistic Weiroll program (mint+approve+swap via MockDEX) to prove the VM still executes end-to-end.
